   "body_snippet": "I am running the latest nightly build, 2022.01.07-1.77.2-nb, and when I try to import from AD I am unable to change to a different domain. After typing in the domain and clicking the Change button nothing happens and the domain list does not switch.\r\n\r\n## Expected Behavior\r\nAfter typing in the domain FQDN and clicking Change I would expect the domain list to refresh showing the OU structure of the specified domain.\r\n\r\n## Current Behavior\r\nNothing happens after typing in the domain FQDN and click...",

+      "date": "2026-02-23",
+      "type": "triaged",
+      "description": "The Active Directory import window's domain textbox is not initialized with the user's current domain, leading to confusion and requiring manual intervention to set the domain."
+    }
+  ],

       "id": "IC_kwDOAAcIMM7mXFZQ",
@@ -43,5 +50,5 @@

+  "notes": "AI triage (gemini): The Active Directory import window's domain textbox is not initialized with the user's current domain, leading to confusion and requiring manual intervention to set the domain.\nApproach: Initialize txtDomain.Text with Environment.UserDomainName in the constructor of ActiveDirectoryImportWindow.cs."
 }

+      "type": "triaged",
+      "description": "The SSH session window (PuTTYNG) does not automatically adjust its size when the parent mRemoteNG window is resized, requiring an update to the process control and window management."
+    }
+  ],

       "id": "IC_kwDOAAcIMM4_b-7p",
@@ -60,5 +67,5 @@

-  "notes": ""
+  "notes": "AI triage (gemini): The SSH session window (PuTTYNG) does not automatically adjust its size when the parent mRemoteNG window is resized, requiring an update to the process control and window management.\nApproach: Add a method to `ProcessController.cs` to resize the external PuTTYNG window using P/Invoke (SetWindowPos/MoveWindow from `NativeMethods.cs`). Override the resize event in the relevant mRemoteNG UI window (e.g., `BaseWindow` descendant) to get the new dimensions and pass them to the `ProcessController`'s resize method."
 }
